title: Sean Penn Wins 2026 Oscar for Best Supporting Actor
text: Sean Penn won the Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor for his role in One Battle After Another, marking his third Oscar victory. However, the acclaimed actor was absent from the Dolby Theater ceremony. Penn's competitors in the category included his co-star Benicio del Toro, Jacob Elordi (Frankenstein), Delroy Lindo (Sinners), and Stellan Skarsgård (Sentimental Value). Presenter Kieran Culkin acknowledged Penn's absence, stating: Sean Penn couldn't be here tonight, or didn't want to, so I'll be accepting this award on his behalf. According to reporting from The New York Times, Penn was en route to Europe and Ukraine at the time of the ceremony. This marks the second major awards show Penn skipped this season—he also missed both the Screen Actors Guild Awards and BAFTA ceremonies, though he did attend the Golden Globes in January where he won the same category. In One Battle After Another, directed by Paul Thomas Anderson, Penn portrayed villain Colonel Steven J. Lockjaw. Penn had been considered the frontrunner, with prediction markets giving him approximately an 80% chance of winning. Penn's previous Oscar wins were for Mystic River (2003) and Milk (2008). Notably, he donated his Mystic River Oscar to Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in 2022.
